name: llama.cpp
description: High-performance local LLM inference engine with Intel Arc GPU support via SYCL.
author: AURINI project

dependencies.name: Intel oneAPI
dependencies.description: Required for Intel Arc GPU acceleration via SYCL. Install 'intel-deep-learning-essentials' (recommended) or 'intel-basekit' from Intel's apt repository.

checks.description: Current user is in the 'render' group (required for Intel GPU access at runtime)
Current user is in the 'video' group (required for Intel GPU access at runtime)
Enough disk space to clone and build llama.cpp (minimum 5 GB)
Internet connection available (needed to clone or update from GitHub)
Intel Arc GPU is visible via sycl-ls (confirms drivers and oneAPI are working)
Install path is writable by the current user
checks.note: Path is set by the user during configure. Checked at pre-flight time against the chosen path.

remedies.description: Intel oneAPI must be installed manually.
instructions: echo 'deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/oneapi-archive-keyring.gpg] https://apt.repos.intel.com/oneapi all main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/oneAPI.list
Then install (recommended — smaller, has everything llama.cpp needs):

remedies.description: Add the current user to the 'render' group. A logout and login is required for the change to take effect.
remedies.undo_instructions: sudo gpasswd -d $USER render — then log out and back in.

remedies.description: Add the current user to the 'video' group. A logout and login is required for the change to take effect.
remedies.undo_instructions: sudo gpasswd -d $USER video — then log out and back in.

remedies.description: The Intel Arc GPU is not visible to the oneAPI stack.
instructions: · Intel GPU drivers not installed — try: sudo apt install intel-gpu-tools level-zero
· User not in 'render' or 'video' groups — see checks above
· oneAPI installation incomplete — try reinstalling intel-deep-learning-essentials
You can continue the build without confirming GPU visibility, but llama.cpp
may not use your GPU when you try to run it.

remedies.description: The install directory is not writable. This usually means it was created by a previous run with sudo. Fix ownership so the current user can write to it.
remedies.undo_instructions: The original owner was root. To restore: sudo chown -R root:root {install_path}

settings.label: FP16 compute
settings.description: Halves the VRAM used for compute buffers with negligible quality impact. Strongly recommended for Arc A750, A770, and other 8 GB cards. Has no effect on model weight quality — only intermediate GPU calculations.

settings.label: Flash Attention
settings.description: Reduces VRAM usage during inference by computing attention more efficiently. Added recently — performance varies by model. Recommended for 8 GB cards running larger models.

settings.label: Context size
settings.description: Maximum number of tokens the model can work with at once. Higher values use more VRAM. 4096 is a safe default for 8 GB cards.

settings.label: GPU layers
settings.description: Number of model layers to offload to the GPU. Set to 99 to offload as many as possible (recommended). Reduce if you run out of VRAM.

settings.label: Model
settings.description: Path to the GGUF model file to load.

settings.label: Host
settings.description: Address llama-server listens on. Use 127.0.0.1 to keep it local (recommended). Use 0.0.0.0 to allow connections from other devices on your network.

settings.label: CPU threads
settings.description: Number of CPU threads to use. Defaults to half the available threads — a safe balance between speed and system responsiveness.
settings.note: Defaults to half of os.cpu_count() at launch time if not set.

notes: icx/icpx must be passed explicitly even after sourcing setvars.sh.
Without them cmake may silently fall back to gcc/g++ and produce a broken SYCL build.
-DGGML_SYCL_F16 is controlled by the fp16 setting and added at build time.
-DGGML_SYCL_FLASH_ATTN is a build-time flag but flash_attn is a launch flag in llama.cpp — do not add it here.
